records.ety: [F. signifier, L. significare; signum a sign + -ficare (in comp.) to make. See Sign, n., and -fy.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Signified (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Signifying (?).]

defs: To show by a sign; to communicate by any conventional token, as words, gestures, signals, or the like; to announce; to make known; to declare; to express; as, a signified his desire to be present.
quotes: I 'll to the king; and signify to him That thus I have resign'd my charge to you.
The government should signify to the Protestants of Ireland that want of silver is not to be remedied.

defs: To mean; to import; to denote; to betoken.
quotes: He bade her tell him what it signified.
A tale Told by an idiot, full of sound and fury, Signifying nothing.
notes: ☞ Signify is often used impersonally; as, it signifies nothing, it does not signify, that is, it is of no importance.
